behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is deterministic key-derivation logic (normalization/serialization/hash) inside one component. No I/O required; mocks do not materially hide this class of bug.
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit tests on the key generator: (a) same logical params with reordered map fields produce identical key, (b) distinct params across many randomized pairs produce different keys, and (c) edge cases (nulls, arrays, Unicode, numeric/string distinctions) remain stable.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often occurs at config-to-runtime wiring (parsing defaults, unit conversion, category mapping), which pure unit tests of formula logic can miss.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test loading real cache config and TTL calculator together: assert category-to-TTL mapping (e.g., user_profiles=300s, product_catalog=3600s), verify unknown category fallback behavior, and verify config reload/override does not mis-scale units.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real process memory/OS reporting behavior and threshold-trigger mechanics. In-process mocks of memory metrics can hide production failures in measurement source, polling cadence, or unit mismatch.
behaviors.test_description: System test runs cache service process with configured threshold, drives memory growth using realistic allocations, and asserts observed strategy transitions from LRU to LFU in runtime metrics/logs. Include boundary checks just below/above threshold.

behaviors.description: A write to PostgreSQL invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real DB write semantics and invalidation plumbing (triggers/CDC/listeners/transactions). Mocked DB events frequently miss race, transaction visibility, and payload-shape issues.
behaviors.test_description: System test with real PostgreSQL and cache service: seed rows and corresponding cached entries, perform INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE in real transactions, then assert only matching keys are invalidated after commit (and not before), including multi-row updates.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries under LRU and LFU when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple cache modules (admission, bookkeeping, eviction state) working together. Pure unit tests of selector functions can miss state-update bugs across real cache operations, but no OS/network dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test against in-memory cache implementation with fixed capacity: run controlled access sequences, force full condition, and assert evicted key set matches expected under LRU and LFU separately (including tie-break behavior and counter aging/reset rules if defined).
